channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Iran's cultural heritage sites are being damaged by American and Israeli strikes
item.description: The cost of the joint U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran are mounting in terms of civilian deaths and damage to Iran's most famous cultural heritage sites.
item.content:encoded: The cost of the joint U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran are mounting in terms of civilian deaths and damage to Iran's most famous cultural heritage sites.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: 'Everybody was wearing black.' How the Iranian diaspora is observing Nowruz amid war
item.description: Nowruz celebrates the arrival of spring and rebirth. But for many in the Iranian diaspora, this year is different. As the war continues, many are trying to balance the joy of the holiday with grief.
item.content:encoded:
haft sin — a traditional table set for Nowruz featuring symbolic objects.'/>

Nowruz celebrates the arrival of spring and rebirth. But for many in the Iranian diaspora, this year is different. As the war continues, many are trying to balance the joy of the holiday with grief.

(Image credit: Sarah Ventre)
item.dc:creator: Sarah Ventre

item.title: How the Iran war threatens global food supply
item.description: About a third of all fertilizer shipped globally passes through the Strait of Hormuz. Now shipping is all-but stopped through the Strait and this could have repercussions for the global food supply.
item.content:encoded:
About a third of all fertilizer shipped globally passes through the Strait of Hormuz. Now shipping is all-but stopped through the Strait and this could have repercussions for the global food supply.

(Image credit: Narinder Nanu/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis

item.title: Palestinians celebrate Eid in Gaza, making the most of a fragile ceasefire
item.description: The ceasefire, in effect for the past six months, has brought some reprieve to Palestinians in Gaza despite continued hardship, displacement and Israeli restrictions on aid.
item.content:encoded:
The ceasefire, in effect for the past six months, has brought some reprieve to Palestinians in Gaza despite continued hardship, displacement and Israeli restrictions on aid.

(Image credit: Anas Baba)
item.dc:creator: Anas Baba

item.title: Trump is dismantling democracy, reports find. And, Treasury to take over student loans
item.description: Recent studies show the U.S. is slipping further from democracy. And, the Trump administration plans to transfer federal student loans from the Education Department to the Treasury Department.
item.content:encoded:
Recent studies show the U.S. is slipping further from democracy. And, the Trump administration plans to transfer federal student loans from the Education Department to the Treasury Department.

(Image credit: Jim Watson)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Global oil and energy prices are reeling as the war in the Middle East escalates
item.description: Israel and Iran continue to exchange airstrikes with a focus on energy infrastructure. President Trump says Israel acted alone in striking a key gas field and better not do it again.
item.content:encoded: Israel and Iran continue to exchange airstrikes with a focus on energy infrastructure. President Trump says Israel acted alone in striking a key gas field and better not do it again.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Kahn

item.title: Trump's mediators offer Hamas a formal proposal to give up its weapons in Gaza
item.description: President Trump's Board of Peace has quietly given Hamas a proposal to hand over all of its weapons to ensure Gaza's reconstruction.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's Board of Peace has quietly given Hamas a proposal to hand over all of its weapons to ensure Gaza's reconstruction.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Trump says he mulls 'winding down' the Iran war, even as more Marines head to Mideast
item.description: The USS Boxer, carrying thousands of Marines, has left California and will reach the Persian Gulf in about three weeks. This, as the energy crisis pushes the Trump administration to lift sanctions on Iranian oil stranded at sea.
item.content:encoded:
The USS Boxer, carrying thousands of Marines, has left California and will reach the Persian Gulf in about three weeks. This, as the energy crisis pushes the Trump administration to lift sanctions on Iranian oil stranded at sea.

(Image credit: Gregory Bull)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: From mall to torture site: Venezuela debates El Helicoide prison's future
item.description: Once a futuristic shopping mall, El Helicoide became one of Venezuela's most feared prisons. Now, as the country changes, so does its fate — erase it, rebuild it, or remember what happened inside.
item.content:encoded:
Once a futuristic shopping mall, El Helicoide became one of Venezuela's most feared prisons. Now, as the country changes, so does its fate — erase it, rebuild it, or remember what happened inside.

(Image credit: Ariana Cubillos)
item.dc:creator: Manuel Rueda

item.title: Why it's so hard for world leaders to bring down oil and gasoline prices
item.description: From waiving the Jones Act to rerouting oil through the Red Sea, governments are doing their best to make up for the crisis in the Strait of Hormuz, but prices are still rising.
item.content:encoded:
From waiving the Jones Act to rerouting oil through the Red Sea, governments are doing their best to make up for the crisis in the Strait of Hormuz, but prices are still rising.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske
